title: terminal-poc-corpus PoC record
description: Metadata + attribution for one terminal-attack proof-of-concept. The payload itself lives hex-encoded in the sibling payload.hex, never in this file.

id.description: Stable slug; must equal the containing directory name.

archive_url.description: Web-archive snapshot of source_url, or the literal 'TODO' until captured.

date.description: Disclosure / publication date (ISO 8601, year or full).

payload_encoding.description: Payloads are ALWAYS hex at rest (read-safe). See SAFETY.md.

expected_effect.description: Worded description of what a vulnerable terminal does with the payload.

verification.description: How a hit is detected. canary-command: a fired injection runs the safe canary command (writes the marker). display-deception: the rendered text differs from the logical text (bidi/homoglyph). clipboard-exfil: the payload silently sets the clipboard to the canary token. hyperlink-mismatch: the visible link text differs from its target. Non-command modes carry no shell command, so they are inherently harmless.

canary.description: The unique marker token used to detect a hit for this verification mode (the command's marker file, the clipboard token, the reordered/homoglyph token, or the phishing target marker).

modified.description: true if the corpus adapted the upstream PoC (e.g. canary-forked a destructive action). Anything with a harmful original MUST be true.

original_ref.description: Pointer to the unmodified upstream form when modified is true.
